title: pimelea axiflora subsp. alpina
intro: Pimelea axiflora subsp. alpina, also known as alpine rice flower, is a subalpine shrub native to Tasmania and southeastern Australia, characterized by its small white or pinkish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pimelea axiflora subsp. alpina include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the need for proper identification and preparation.
